stimulus: Before the Panama Canal opened in 1914, ships traveling between New York and San Francisco had to sail around the southern tip of South America, a journey of more than two months. By connecting the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific ______blank canal reduced travel time to just a few weeks.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of a comma to set off an introductory prepositional/participial phrase from the main clause. A comma is needed after "the Pacific Ocean" to separate the introductory phrase ("By connecting...the Pacific Ocean") from the main clause ("the canal reduced travel time...").

Choice A is incorrect because a semicolon can't separate an introductory phrase from a main clause. Choice B is incorrect because an opening parenthesis is unmatched and results in an ungrammatical sentence. Choice C is incorrect because a colon can't be used in this way.
